alerts.title: Iran warns Trump: cease Lebanon war or face unannounced Israeli strike
alerts.summary: Drop Site News reports Iran has issued direct warning to Trump administration: if US-Israeli operations against Lebanon continue, Iran may execute unannounced strike on Israel (incidents feed, 2026-06-19). Warning signals potential escalation beyond proxy exchanges to direct Iranian retaliation, raising imminent conflict risk.

alerts.title: US domestic political fracture deepens over Iran deal durability
alerts.summary: US sentiment deteriorates to 44% positive / 48% negative. 'Trump' mentions spike to 87 (up from 83), with 'Meloni' (22), 'Obama' (19), and 'Vance' (9) appearing as criticism of missile arsenal concession spreads across CNN, Jerusalem Post, Guardian (sentiment feed). Domestic polarization now threatens unilateral deal withdrawal.

alerts.title: Iran enrichment stable at 60% U-235; disposal bottleneck emerges
alerts.summary: Global metrics confirm Iran maintaining 60% U-235 enrichment with 1-2 week breakout window. New development: leaked MoU text reveals agreement permits enriched uranium stockpile to remain in-country for dilution rather than export, creating verification and technical disposal challenges (FDD, Boston Herald, Jerusalem Post, RFE/RL). Disposal logistics now central vulnerability in deal implementation.

alerts.title: Three US destroyers maintain Red Sea posture amid Houthi escalation
alerts.summary: Assets feed confirms USS Carney, USS Mason, and USS Laboon deployed in Red Sea (lat 15.3-15.7°N, lon 41.6-42.5°E) alongside USS Dwight D. Eisenhower carrier group. Positioning concurrent with incidents feed reporting Houthis attacking Israel and announcing ban on Israeli vessels in Red Sea, maintaining naval deterrent amid proxy maritime activity.

alerts.title: Houthis escalate Red Sea operations; Israeli shipping restrictions declared
alerts.summary: Incidents feed reports Houthis attack Israel and announce formal ban on Israeli vessels transiting Red Sea (2026-06-19, The Long War Journal). Move signals hardening of proxy maritime posture independent of ceasefire dynamics in Lebanon, potentially disrupting commercial traffic and signaling capability to interdict shipping in strategic waterway.

analysis_note: US-Iran nuclear deal faces simultaneous pressure from Iranian direct-strike warnings, domestic US political unraveling, technical verification bottlenecks, and proxy maritime escalation; Israel's continued Lebanon operations threaten deal's survival amid Trump administration fracture over deal terms.
